nostalgiaetherealcollection_file77.episode: The Best Of One Step Beyond's Season One.mp4
nostalgiaetherealcollection_file77.desc:
Welcome to nostalgia central. NostalgiaEthereal's aka The Retro Connoisseur's wide collection of nostalgic content all in one place for everyone caring to go back in time for hours upon hours. VHS recordings ranging from the 90's to 00's, long marathons of obscure television, vintage cartoons and anime, retro drive-in movie projects, TV-movie broadcast recreations, original commercials throughout, Adult Swim-related content, and September 11th-related content. This is a time-machine in the form of a database. As time progresses, more will be added as I acquire more VHS tapes to transfer. So be on the lookout as time moves on for new content uploaded. Archiving these things is HIGHLY important and crucial to preservation of a time period that has long past and must be kept alive through data-hoarding archived media catalogues such as this.
I do not own any of the content shared here. Only for historical preservation and entertainment purposes.

HansRuediGiger-Biomechanical-Repository_.show: H.R. Giger Biomechanical Repository of Shadows
HansRuediGiger-Biomechanical-Repository_.episode: H.R. Giger Biomechanical Repository of Shadows/H. R. Giger Documentaries/Features and featurettes/Dark Star - H.R. Giger's World Journey into the Surreal (2014)/Dark Star - H.R. Giger's World Journey into the Surreal.ia.mp4
HansRuediGiger-Biomechanical-Repository_.desc: "Some people would say my paintings show a future world and maybe they do, but I paint from reality. I put several things and ideas together, and perhaps, when I have finished, it could show the future." - H. R. Giger --------------------- Biography: Hans Ruedi Giger, born on February 5, 1940, in Chur, Switzerland, was a visionary artist renowned for his unique and surreal biomechanical style. Giger's fascination with the macabre and the fantastical can be traced back to his early childhood nightmares, which later fueled his artistic endeavors. He studied architecture and industrial design at the School of Applied Arts in Zurich, where he honed his skills in various mediums. Giger's work is characterized by a fusion of organic and mechanical elements, creating haunting yet mesmerizing landscapes and creatures. Giger gained international acclaim for his work on the film Alien (1979), directed by Ridley Scott. His design of the titular creature earned him an Academy Award for Best Visual Effects. The success of Alien propelled Giger into the spotlight, leading to collaborations with various filmmakers and musicians. His distinctive style also influenced a wide range of media, from video games to album covers, most notably for bands like Emerson, Lake & Palmer and Debbie Harry. Throughout his career, Giger continued to explore themes of birth, death, and the subconscious. His art often evoked a sense of unease, yet it captivated audiences with its intricate detail and originality. Giger passed away on May 12, 2014, in Zurich, leaving behind a legacy that continues to inspire artists and creators worldwide.
HansRuediGiger-Biomechanical-Repository_.u: https://archive.org/download/HansRuediGiger-Biomechanical-Repository/H.R. Giger Biomechanical Repository of Shadows/H. R. trauma-survivor-reacts-to-moral-orel-par.show: Trauma Survivor Reacts To Moral Orel Part 3
trauma-survivor-reacts-to-moral-orel-par.desc: In Part 3 of this emotional reaction series, I dive deep into Moral Orel once again. As a trauma survivor, the show’s exploration of childhood neglect, dysfunctional families, and religious trauma hits even harder. This isn’t just a typical reaction video; it’s a raw, unfiltered look at how these themes resonate with my own experiences. Whether you're dealing with your own trauma or just want to explore how media reflects the darkness of human experience, this video is for you. Join me as I process and unpack the emotional weight of Moral Orel and share the impact it has on me personally. Please like, share, and subscribe to stay updated on this vulnerable, healing journey. Let’s continue to break the silence on difficult topics together.
